symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden profound central cyanosis (dark blue lips and mucosa) triggered by straining

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Hypercyanotic (Tet) spell in Tetralogy of Fallot
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudden central cyanosis precipitated by a Valsalva strain in a known-CHD infant is the defining feature of a hypercyanotic spell.
Cyanosis out of proportion to respiratory findings and without fever points away from a primary chest infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe respiratory distress — very fast gasping breathing with chest recession

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Hypercyanotic (Tet) spell in Tetralogy of Fallot
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tachypnoea and hyperpnoea are part of the spell as the infant tries to compensate for profound hypoxia.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Hypercyanotic (Tet) spell in Tetralogy of Fallot
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A spell that will not break spontaneously marks a severe, potentially fatal event demanding immediate intervention.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced level of interaction / decreasing responsiveness with diaphoresis

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Hypercyanotic (Tet) spell in Tetralogy of Fallot
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A falling conscious level in a hypoxic infant is a pre-terminal cerebral-hypoxia sign requiring emergency response.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known congenital heart defect (VSD + murmur) with poor feeding and failure to thrive, awaiting surgery

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Hypercyanotic (Tet) spell in Tetralogy of Fallot
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The pre-existing structural defect is the substrate that makes a hypercyanotic spell possible.

symptoms.symptom_name: Infant held lying flat on his back

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Hypercyanotic (Tet) spell in Tetralogy of Fallot
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Not diagnostic, but a modifiable factor — flat positioning worsens the shunt; the correct response is knee-to-chest positioning.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Hypercyanotic (Tet) spell in Tetralogy of Fallot
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective severity markers — clinician-measured; the AI Doctor infers critical hypoxia from visible cyanosis and distress, not from a measured SpO2.

symptoms.symptom_name: Previously loud systolic murmur now diminished on auscultation

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Hypercyanotic (Tet) spell in Tetralogy of Fallot
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A quietening murmur during a spell reflects near-total RVOT obstruction with maximal right-to-left shunt — a sealed auscultation finding unavailable via telehealth.
Clubbing indicates chronic cyanotic congenital heart disease; a clinician examination finding, not obtainable in this acute telehealth moment.
No fever reduces the likelihood of a primary chest infection as the cause of the cyanosis and distress.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Hypercyanotic (Tet) spell in Tetralogy of Fallot
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Sudden central cyanosis + severe distress + prolonged worsening course in a known-CHD infant is a life-threatening hypercyanotic spell requiring immediate knee-to-chest positioning and 000.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency ambulance transfer with continuous SpO2 and cardiac monitoring
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The definitive next step is emergency transport to a paediatric-capable ED; monitoring and oxygen are provided en route by paramedics.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Pulse oximetry (if a home device is on hand)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: May confirm profound hypoxia, but must NEVER delay knee-to-chest positioning and calling 000; visible cyanosis is already sufficient to act.
Hospital-based confirmation and surgical planning; not a telehealth action but the correct downstream pathway.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Chest auscultation / routine outpatient work-up before escalation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Attempting remote examination or deferring for routine tests instead of emergency escalation would dangerously delay life-saving care.
